Name: pac_bayes_latency_critical_inference
Title: PAC-Bayes Frameworks for Latency-Critical Inference with Spectral Stability Bounds and Jacobian-Based Layer-Skipping
Short Hypothesis: By integrating PAC-Bayes frameworks with spectral stability bounds and Jacobian-based layer-skipping, we can develop a novel approach for latency-critical inference that provides rigorous guarantees on the trade-off between accuracy and latency.
Related Work: Recent works such as SNIP, AMP-ViT, and EMQ have focused on adaptive mixed-precision training and quantization for efficient inference. However, these methods often rely on heuristic-based approaches or require significant computational overhead. In contrast, our proposal leverages PAC-Bayes frameworks and spectral stability bounds to provide a more principled and efficient approach for latency-critical inference.
Abstract: We propose a novel approach for latency-critical inference that integrates PAC-Bayes frameworks with spectral stability bounds and Jacobian-based layer-skipping. Our approach provides rigorous guarantees on the trade-off between accuracy and latency, allowing for efficient and reliable deployment of machine learning models in latency-critical applications. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through extensive experiments on benchmark datasets, showcasing significant improvements in latency and accuracy compared to state-of-the-art baselines.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed approach on benchmark datasets such as ImageNet and CIFAR-10
Compare the performance of our approach with state-of-the-art baselines such as SNIP and AMP-ViT
Investigate the impact of different hyperparameters and layer-skipping strategies on the trade-off between accuracy and latency
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed approach may require significant computational overhead for training and deployment
The effectiveness of the approach may depend on the specific dataset and application
The integration of PAC-Bayes frameworks and spectral stability bounds may require careful tuning of hyperparameters
